title: China says it is not the ‘root cause’ of the EU’s troubles following talks
summary: China’s large — and growing — trade surplus with the 27-nation bloc has increasingly raised concerns across Europe, putting pressure on Brussels to protect local industries. As the EU opts for more negotiations, the PRC senses and opening to divide the EU internally.

title: Xi Casts China’s Ruling Party as Force for Global Prosperity
summary: Marking the Communist Party's 105th anniversary, Xi Jinping described it as a builder of world peace and global development, pledging military modernization and reiterating ambitions for "national rejuvenation," including resolving the "Taiwan problem." He avoided naming the U.S. directly but referenced facing a "treacherous path." The speech reflects Xi's push to position China, via forums like BRICS, as a...

title: China tells its ethnic minorities to integrate or face consequences with sweeping new unity law
summary: Beijing's new Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law, effective July 1, mandates Mandarin-language education, Communist Party loyalty in curricula, and integration-focused housing policy across China's 56 ethnic groups. It also asserts authority to penalize individuals and organizations abroad accused of undermining ethnic unity, raising fears of transnational repression targeting diaspora communities, researchers...

title: World Bank to phase out China lending
summary: The World Bank which is designed to lend money to poor countries will finally end its lending to the PRC, the world’s second largest economy by 2031. Washington is urging other institutions, including the Asian Development Bank, to follow suit. China says it will keep cooperating with the World Bank on developing-country initiatives.

title: What to Know About How Iran Uses China’s Currency to Weaken U.S. Power
summary: A WSJ investigation details how Iran increasingly settles trade through China's yuan-based CIPS payment system rather than dollar channels, evading U.S. sanctions oversight. China buys over 90% of Iran's crude, paying via intermediaries that fund Chinese contractors or exporters rather than direct transfers. Analysts say China isn't trying to replace the dollar but is building sanctions-resistant trade corridors...

title: Politics chat: Trump's July 4 speech, financial disclosures; Supreme Court rulings
summary: We take a look at President Trump's July 4th speech, as well as his reluctance to sign the bipartisan housing bill intended to help make homes affordable for Americans.

title: Paul Pelosi in hit-and-run in California, car left with major damage, authorities say
summary: The husband of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi was involved in a hit-and-run in California that left a parked car with "major" damage, authorities said Saturday.

title: Trump's administration won't seek new bids to repair the Reflecting Pool
summary: Like President Donald Trump, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said he was 100% sure that vandals caused the damage to the century-old Reflecting Pool on the National Mall.

title: WATCH: Trump delivers keynote address at 'Salute to America 250' event on the National Mall
summary: Speaking in Washington after storms prompted a roughly two-hour evacuation of the National Mall, Trump honored veterans, including several from World War II and one of the first Black officers to lead a Special Forces team in combat in Vietnam.

title: Shapiro: Trump ‘accumulated way more power than our founders would have hoped’
summary: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro (D) on Sunday said following President Trump's Independence Day address that the commander in chief has “accumulated” more power than the founding fathers would have hoped for, echoing consistent criticism from Democrats. “Only more recently do we have an executive who, again, I think has accumulated way more power than our...

title: Hegseth goes quiet on Iran as Trump pursues diplomacy
summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth was the Trump administration’s frontman for waging war on Iran during weeks of kinetic operations aimed at decimating its nuclear threat. Since President Trump signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Iran to end the war, without securing significant concessions on its missile or nuclear stockpiles, Hegseth has said little about...

title: Mike Johnson on democratic socialist candidates: 'There are many Mamdani's popping up'
summary: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said "there are many Mamdani's popping up," referring to New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani (D) in talking about democratic socialists winning recent Democratic state primaries. Johnson echoed President Trump's claim that "communism" is a threat to the country. "There are many Mamdanis popping up, running for Congress around...

title: Live updates: Trump, nation celebrate America 250
summary: President Trump marked the nation's 250th birthday with a late Saturday night speech that blended the championing of American achievements with partisan rhetoric. Trump's address lasted about 30 minutes — not as long as he promised days before extreme heat and weather impacted the Fourth of July celebrations in Washington on Saturday. As the president...

title: To unlock agentic AI's promise for government, America must build reliability
summary: The promise of agentic AI is massive — the problem is we cannot currently deploy agentic AI at scale, because today's systems are not yet reliable enough.

title: Navy Ends Search for Missing Crew Member After Arabian Sea Helicopter Crash
summary: The cause of the crash was still under investigation, but the military said it was not the result of hostile fire. A total of 14 U.S. service members have been killed in the war against Iran.

title: Bill Archer, Influential Texas Congressman, Is Dead at 98
summary: Serving for 30 years, he eventually wrote virtually all tax laws considered by the House and was a major influence on budget bills as Ways and Means chairman.

title: On America’s 250th, Smithsonian Museums Offer Respite and Reflection
summary: An institution under attack from the Trump administration provided relief from the weather on July 4 — and a chance to ponder what it means to be American.

title: Trump Administration Rolls Back Dozens of Gun Regulations
summary: Critics say the administration is weakening public safety. Proponents say regulations would be where they were before President Joseph R. Biden took office.

title: At Trump’s Direction, Federal Agencies Are Abandoning Discrimination Cases
summary: President Trump has tried to scale back anti-discrimination regulations that date back decades. Federal agencies have heeded his call.

title: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

title: US marks 250th birthday with fireworks, flyovers and extreme weather
summary: US President Donald Trump's speech included some of his political agenda but also honoured war veterans and American history.

summary: Some of the famous guests have been posting on social media about the big day in New York.

title: Evacuations in Guam as super typhoon Bavi approaches
summary: The storm is forecast to bring winds in excess of 160mph and waves nearly 11m high when it makes landfall on Monday.

summary: Today's Independence Day celebrations may be tamped down by severe heat and, according to a NPR/PBS News/Marist poll, worry about the future.

title: 'Cautiously optimistic': Swing voters describe their view of America at 250
summary: As America turns 250, voters from our Swing Shift project talk about the state of the country. Their views ranged from "uncertain" and "concerned" to "excited" and "cautiously optimistic."

title: WATCH: Trump hails U.S. exceptionalism, warns of communist 'threat' in speech at Mount Rushmore
summary: While the language was similar to several other speeches Trump has given in recent days, it was notable for being delivered in a national park that commemorates some of America's most prominent presidents.

title: The Supreme Court, Mail-in Voting, Campaign Spending, and the 2026 Midterm Elections
summary: The Supreme Court’s decision this week on mail-in ballots got the most attention, but its decision on campaign spending may have a bigger impact on the fight for control of Congress.

title: A Year Later, Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill Act Has a Clean Tech Silver Lining
summary: Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act gutted the wind and solar credits at the heart of Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act. But a year on, the law’s preserved incentives for geothermal, advanced nuclear, and grid-scale storage may do more to cut global emissions than the credits it eliminated.

title: Behind the curve: Britain’s defence investment plan in Trump’s world
summary: The new defence investment plan is weighed down by the habits of Britain’s defence establishment. By contrast, the British public appear readier to accept change.

title: Regional roots, global decisions: Andy Burnham’s foreign policy challenge
summary: Andy Burnham, Britain’s likely next prime minister, is clear-eyed about his domestic political priorities. But, with the world order in flux, all eyes are on his foreign policy approach

title: The future is fermented: How Europe can succeed in the next industrial race
summary: The next industrial revolution will be built with microbes as much as machines. China understands this. Europe still has the science to compete but must act quickly before industrial leadership slips away again

title: A Belgian diamond group is gifting Trump a lavish ring after that industry won tariff relief
summary: It's a glitzy window into the role that ostentatious – and almost always gilded — gifts are playing by those seeking to curry favor with the U.S. president.

title: Marriage Penalties and Bonuses in the Federal Tax Code
summary: When a couple marries, their federal income tax payments are likely to be impacted. Marriage may cause a couple’s tax payments to increase, resulting in a marriage tax penalty, or decrease, resulting in a marriage tax bonus. Marriage penalties and bonuses emerge because of the impossibility of balancing three competing goals in the income tax code: progressivity (higher-income taxpayers pay higher marginal tax...

title: Trump v. Slaughter and the Future of For-Cause Removal Protections
summary: In a 6-3 decision on June 29, 2026, the Supreme Court held that statutory provisions that allow the President to remove Federal Trade Commission (FTC) commissioners only “for cause” violate the Constitution. title: Washington's Growing Portfolio: Tracking U.S. Government Investments
summary: CFR’s U.S. Government Deal Tracker shows how the government is experimenting with new tools and financing structures to advance a range of strategic sectors, including critical minerals, energy, global logistics, manufacturing, telecommunications, and other technologies.

title: Farm Bill Primer: Programs Without a Budget Baseline
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) provides budgetary projections of government spending called baselines. Following rules of both House and Senate budget committees, CBO assumes that many farm bill programs that receive mandatory funding will continue beyond the end of their authorization. That is, they have a continuing baseline beyond the end of a farm bill that gives them built-in future funding if Congress...

title: How Schools and After-School Programs Can Collaborate to Support Academic Alignment
summary: The authors provide national data about the state of academic alignment between school-day and after-school learning, illustrate how schools and after-school programs put academic alignment into practice, and identify enabling and impeding factors.

title: Military Effectiveness Should Be Determined by The War Department Not Judges
summary: The military isn't a therapeutic institution; its purpose is to deter war, fight war, and win war. There are hard truths in military life that a humane society may dislike but cannot repeal. The first is that military service is not an entitlement. It is a calling, yes; an honor, certainly. But it is also a profession whose members must be prepared to deploy, fight, endure privation, and, if necessary, die in...

title: America’s Asian Allies Want Nuclear Submarines. Here’s How To Build Them
summary: Nuclear power is cool once again and is all the rage in Asia. The North Koreans are getting in on the action with the rumored development of a nuclear-powered submarine. title: Don't Regulate Away America's Medical Device Sterilization Industry
summary: In 2024, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) tried to make it harder for hospitals to obtain essential sterilized medical equipment by threatening to regulate the American industry out of business. title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.
